papers.title: The Scalability of Transformer Attention: A Critical Survey of Long-Context Mechanisms

annotations.quote: The dot-product attention operator of Vaswani et al.
annotations.contextBefore: shaped a decade of architecture research. Introduction
annotations.contextAfter: computes, for a sequence of length n and head dimension d, an n x n matrix of pairwise scores.
annotations.note: The citation is here, but the operator deserves the one-line statement of its cost up front so Section 2 does not carry the whole definition.

annotations.quote: It is sometimes claimed that linear attention is equivalent to softmax attention in the limit of an exact feature map.
annotations.contextBefore: the feature map's positivity assumptions are stressed.
annotations.contextAfter: This is true only in a limit that no deployed feature map approaches
annotations.note: As written this attributes a claim to no one. Either cite the equivalence result you are qualifying or recast it as your own framing.

annotations.quote: Attention is a bottleneck, but not a uniform one.
annotations.contextAfter: A decade of sub-quadratic mechanisms has produced genuine efficiency gains.
annotations.note: Strong opening line for the conclusion — keep it as the first sentence.
